abstract:
This study highlights the synthesis and characterization of various heterocyclic derivatives derived from 1-(2-hydroxyphenyl)-3-(4-methylphenyl)prop-2-en-1-one (chalcone 3) using pressurized microwave irradiation as an efficient green energy source. The reaction of chalcone 3 with ethyl cyanoacetate, acetylacetone, and thioglycolic acid under pressurized microwave conditions at 70 °C yielded 2-hydroxyphenylcyanopyridone, 2-hydroxyphenyl acetylcyclohexanone, and thieno[2,3-c]chromen-4-one, respectively. Additionally, the oxidative cyclization of chalcone 3 with hydrogen peroxide under stirring produced the corresponding chromen-4-one derivative. The chemical structures of all newly synthesized compounds were validated using FT-IR, 1H NMR, 13C NMR, and mass spectrometry. 

Biological evaluation revealed that these heterocycles exhibit exceptional antioxidant activity, comparable to Vitamin C, with the hydroxyl group playing a critical role in enhancing radical scavenging inhibition. Molecular docking simulations of compound 12 against two protein targets (PDB ID: 1DH2 and 3RP8) demonstrated superior binding energy and shorter bond lengths relative to ascorbic acid. Furthermore, density functional theory (DFT/B3LYP/6-31G(d,p)) was employed to optimize the structures and determine their physical descriptors. The structure of compound 12 was definitively confirmed via single-crystal X-ray diffraction and Hirshfeld surface analysis to map hydrogen bonding and electrostatic interactions. Finally, a strong correlation was established between the experimental data (bond lengths, bond angles, FT-IR, and NMR) and the theoretically optimized models.
